description: Initial public version. Market-implied war-end CDF (Polymarket + Manifold) with closest-to-consensus and most-optimistic summary cards; a scrubbable "war in data" timeline of conflict intensity/tone/fire (GDELT + NASA FIRMS), currency vs USD (CBR + NBU), and real GDP and CPI year-on-year for Russia and Ukraine (World Bank Global Economic Monitor + NBU). AI-drafted weekly briefs are auto-published behind integrity guards; reconstructed historical briefs are labelled. Three languages (uk, en, ru), static-first on Cloudflare's free tier.

Added confirmed equipment losses for Russia and Ukraine, from the Oryx visually-confirmed, photo-attributed dataset (CC BY-NC; used non-commercially and credited). Two new cumulative indicators in the "war in data" grid. Personnel losses were considered and deliberately excluded — no neutral, symmetric source exists, so any figure would be a belligerent claim, which the project's no-fabrication rule forbids.

Editorial redesign: centred layout, system-ui type, warm slate palette and bordered cards. The hero CDF is now probability-colour-coded (0% red → 50% blue → 100% green) on the curve, market markers and the two summary cards. "The war in data" became a scrub-by-date indicator grid with Play/Now controls; added an "Aid to Ukraine" indicator (cumulative euros allocated, Kiel Ukraine Support Tracker) and an on-page note explaining why personnel losses are not shown. Language now auto-selects from the browser on first visit and an explicit choice persists in local storage. Methodology and Sources pages updated to match.

Owner decision: Google Analytics 4 added for coarse traffic insight — one third-party script, run cookieless. Under Consent Mode v2 analytics_storage is permanently denied (never granted), so no _ga cookie, no client identifier and no cross-site/cross-session tracking are ever set; consequently there is no consent banner. This still reverses the earlier no-third-party / cookieless-Cloudflare-Analytics posture, so About, the SPEC privacy section and the changelog were rewritten — the site no longer claims to avoid third-party analytics entirely. Also: a Ukrainian-flag favicon and per-locale, build-time Open Graph / Twitter share images that show the current headline and the two live summary cards.
